steps.label: cause
steps.text: A Premier Cru wine comes from a delimited vineyard recognized within the Burgundy hierarchy rather than the broad regional appellation.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: More specific sites and tighter production rules are intended to preserve grapes from vineyards with a stronger record of ripening and expression.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: Premier Cru wines commonly show greater concentration, complexity and ageing capacity than basic regional Burgundy, though producer and vintage remain decisive.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: a more specific classified site and tighter yields can support greater concentration and complexity.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
